_metadata.focus: Window motor / linear actuator RMA triage
personas: OEM customer service rep (Andersen Windows, Marvin)

products.name: iDEA 57mm Linear Actuator - Standard
products.description: 57mm hybrid stepper linear actuator, 200mm travel, IP54

products.name: iDEA 57mm Linear Actuator - High Force
products.description: 57mm hybrid stepper linear actuator, 300mm travel, 2000N peak

products.name: iDEA 28mm Linear Actuator
products.description: 28mm linear actuator, 50mm travel, low-profile window apps

warrantyClaims.subject: Lead screw stripped after 6 months — RMA request
warrantyClaims.description: Customer reports premature lead-screw failure on iDEA 57mm actuator at 6 months in service. Unit installed in vertical sliding window, normal duty cycle (~30 cycles/day). Photo attached shows clear thread shear. Original PO #PO-AND-2025-0814.
warrantyClaims.expectedRationale: In warranty (shipped 2025-08-12, expires 2027-08-12). Failure mode (lead-screw shear) is a known manufacturing defect category. Cycle count well within rated duty. No evidence of misuse.

warrantyClaims.subject: iDEA 28mm motor seized — please replace
warrantyClaims.description: End user (residential) reports motor seized after ~3 years of service. Unit was shipped 2023-04-30. Distributor requesting goodwill replacement.
warrantyClaims.expectedRationale: Out of warranty by 24+ months (warranty expired 2024-04-30, claim filed 2026-04-23). 3 years of service = expected life cycle. Goodwill replacement out of policy without OEM volume relationship — recommend offering paid repair quote.

warrantyClaims.subject: Erratic position feedback — encoder failure?
warrantyClaims.description: Field reports of position drift on iDEA 57-201 after ~7 months. Encoder readings inconsistent with commanded position. May be electrical noise or genuine encoder failure. Customer wants advance replacement; we suspect installation environment (HVAC controls cabinet w/ VFDs nearby).
warrantyClaims.expectedRationale: In warranty, but failure mode could be either (a) encoder defect (covered) or (b) EMI from neighbouring VFDs (installation issue, not covered). Need engineer review of environmental conditions before disposition.

warrantyClaims.subject: Bearing noise — within warranty period
warrantyClaims.description: Customer reports increasing bearing noise on iDEA 43-105 at 22 months. Still functional. Unit shipped 2024-06-18, warranty expires 2025-12-18 — claim filed 4 months past warranty.
warrantyClaims.expectedRationale: Just past warranty (4 months). Bearing wear at 22-26 months can be normal or premature depending on duty cycle. Request usage logs from customer before deciding repair-at-cost vs goodwill repair.

warrantyClaims.subject: Motor not working
warrantyClaims.expectedRationale: In warranty but description has no failure mode, no photos, no error codes, no usage context. Cannot make disposition — request: (1) photo of unit, (2) symptom description, (3) error code if controller-driven, (4) date failure first observed.

warrantyClaims.scenario: Batch quality complaint — manufacturing batch investigation trigger
warrantyClaims.source: REAL — Roche Diagnostics International AG, received via HKP website contact form 2026-05
warrantyClaims.productDescription: STEPPER MOTOR LC1574W-05 13mm PPS Nut — product 09287868001

failureBreakdown.symptom: Motor does not move at all
Motor move with low force — hardware drawer cannot be moved using motor force

warrantyClaims.detectionMethod: Test before use in production line
warrantyClaims.additionalComments: Please check stock to ensure next delivery has no issues.
warrantyClaims.subject: Quality complaint — 13 LC1574W-05-B25 motors defective (4 no movement, 9 low force)
warrantyClaims.description: Roche Diagnostics reports 13 stepper motors from PO 7100328936 (Penang mfg, date codes P2550-P2608) failed pre-production test. 4 units: no movement. 9 units: insufficient pull force — hardware drawer inoperable. Customer requests stock check to prevent further bad deliveries.
warrantyClaims.expectedRationale: Batch complaint with 2 distinct failure modes across 13 units from Penang. Serial pattern (P2550/P2608) suggests manufacturing batch issue. No misuse indicators — detected in test before production use. High-value medical diagnostics OEM. Recommend: (1) approve RMA for all 13, (2) trigger batch investigation on Penang run, (3) hold inventory from same date codes. Auto-escalate to Quality Engineer due to batch size >5 and medical OEM.
warrantyClaims.agentFlowNotes: This scenario demonstrates HKP website contact form parsing (Label: Value lines, no subject line). Intake agent must extract quantity, serial patterns, multi-failure breakdown from free-text description. Batch size >5 + medical OEM = auto-escalate rule.

currentPainQuotes._note: Placeholder until Bill drops Lou Gucciardo transcripts at OneDrive\MSFT Corporate\Customers\AMETEK\Transcripts\HKP-RMA\. These come from the description in the use-case writeup.
currentPainQuotes.intake_email_pain: RMA requests are sent to shared inboxes — quality and operations engineers manually review, decide eligibility, route. Response can take several days due to queue backlogs.
currentPainQuotes.scaling_pain: It works on one person's machine, but we don't know how to scale it globally.
currentPainQuotes.consistency_pain: Outcomes depend on which engineer handles the RMA.
